behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text (pattern/AST/rule evaluation), with no required module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or model output. Higher levels were considered, but behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint rule with template fixtures: one containing phase instructions copied from SKILL.md (must fail with precise diagnostic), one clean template (must pass), and near-miss text (must not false-positive).

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: template interpolation + fixture expansion + final prompt assembly. Unit tests on one formatter can miss leakage introduced during composition. System rejected because behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no dependence on model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-builder pipeline in-process with representative fixtures and interpolation values, then assert assembled prompt excludes phase-instruction leakage patterns while retaining intended user-style content.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is wiring between loader, skill-file resolver, and invocation config. A unit test of one loader function would miss hard-coded fallback behavior in invocation assembly. System considered but rejected: text specifies plugin/skill files, which can be exercised with local test filesystem wiring (no real OS/network edge behavior required). Agentic rejected because this behavior is about sourcing context, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test initializes harness with test plugin/skill directory, runs invocation build, and asserts effective context originates from SKILL.md content (and changes when file content changes) while prompt text remains non-hard-coded.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness orchestration across prompt input, skill-variant selection, and run configuration. Real failure is cross-module state/control handoff, not single-function logic. Agentic considered but rejected because behavior text is capability to run variants "without changing test prompt text," not outcome quality from model output.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test executes two harness runs (baseline skill vs degraded/missing variant) with identical user prompt string, asserting only skill-source input changes between runs and harness accepts both paths.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on whether the real model behavior changes when skill context is degraded. With a stubbed model, this can falsely pass and hide true regressions. Workflow considered but rejected because behavior describes a single behavioral verdict under one prompt, not multiple sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test twice with identical user prompt: baseline SKILL.md and degraded variant. Keep harness/prompt fixed; assert structural behavioral expectation passes baseline and fails degraded variant.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at boundary between model-call client, artifact writer, and parser pipeline ordering. Unit test of parser alone misses ordering bugs. System considered but rejected: local artifact writing/order can be validated in-process with temp filesystem; no real OS primitive or network condition is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test uses a fake model response and instrumented pipeline: assert artifact file/log contains exact raw output before parser invocation, and malformed JSON still preserves raw artifact for diagnostics.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion/validator logic on response objects. This can be caught by direct validator tests with crafted payloads; no mandatory module wiring or live infrastructure. Higher levels rejected because behavior text is about constraint semantics, not runtime integrations.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with synthetic outputs: keyword-present-but-structurally-wrong cases must fail; structurally correct cases must pass; include role-field misplacement and wrong schema-path variants.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and regression detection based on real model outputs. Mocked outputs can hide model-dependent failures. Workflow considered but rejected because text does not require multiple sequential model decisions, only one live execution outcome assessed by stronger assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live-model smoke test with user-style prompt and structural assertions enabled; demonstrate at least one run/case where keyword-only heuristic would pass but structural validator fails, flagging regression.
